creator: david@wood2.org.uk
text:
In message <20070327093007.AF73216A403@hub.freebsd.org>, Edwin Groothuis 
<edwin@FreeBSD.org> writes
>Maintainer of net/freeradius,
>
>Please note that PR ports/110911 has just been submitted.
>
>If it contains a patch for an upgrade, an enhancement or a bug fix
>you agree on, reply to this email stating that you approve the patch
>and a committer will take care of it.

Not approved. On a quick read through, this would work fine - though I 
haven't tested it.

The only reason for not approving this is that I have a version of the 
port in my local Subversion repository that updates the port to 1.1.5, 
fixes a couple of deficiencies, removes various obsolete patches and 
deals with the entire backlog of requests for enhancements.


I'd rather go with my version than approve this patch, because this 
patch updates part of a FreeBSD patch that I've superseded. This patch 
also makes one change to the build system (removing $(LCRYPT) from the 
radiusd: object in src/Main/Makefile.in) which I've only tested in 
conjunction with removing other obsolete patches.

I will, however, use this to double check my work - and I'm very 
grateful for the submission.


I was finishing the PR text when I received one more patch from a user 
on the freeradius-users list. I hope to incorporate that patch, test the 
port and submit the update via a PR hopefully in the next 24 hours (I 
had hoped to do this sooner, but pneumonia intervened!).




David
-- 
David Wood
david@wood2.org.uk
